headline: Microsoft plans $1 billion investment in Thailand, Thai government says
summary: Microsoft plans ‌to invest $1 billion ‌in Thailand over the ​next two years in cloud services and AI infrastructure, ‌the ⁠Thai government said in a ⁠statement.

headline: Nebius furthers European expansion with $10 billion AI data centre in Finland
summary: AI infrastructure firm Nebius Group on Tuesday said it was continuing its rapid European ‌expansion with a 310-megawatt data centre in Finland, which would be one ‌of the continent's largest with an estimated value of more than $10 billion as demand for AI computing ​grows.  The Amsterdam-based company said capacity at its 10th site, which Finnish developer Polarnode is already building in Lappeenranta near Finland's eastern border with Russia, is due to come online in phases from 2027.  Nebius has recently won supply contracts worth more than $40 billion ‌in total with U.S. software ⁠giants Microsoft and Meta.

content.title: Nebius furthers European expansion with $10 billion AI data centre in Finland
content.summary: AI infrastructure firm Nebius Group on Tuesday said it was continuing its rapid European ‌expansion with a 310-megawatt data centre in Finland, which would be one ‌of the continent's largest with an estimated value of more than $10 billion as demand for AI computing ​grows.  The Amsterdam-based company said capacity at its 10th site, which Finnish developer Polarnode is already building in Lappeenranta near Finland's eastern border with Russia, is due to come online in phases from 2027.  Nebius has recently won supply contracts worth more than $40 billion ‌in total with U.S. software ⁠giants Microsoft and Meta.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: Nebius launches its first AI UK data centre, a new facility hosting NVIDIA and other computer firms, at Ark Data Centres, in Chertsey

headline: Big Tech's $635 billion AI spending faces energy shock test, S&P Global says
summary: Massive investments in artificial intelligence that underpinned record runs in equities face a major ‌hurdle as the Middle East crisis clouds prospects for growth ‌and energy costs, said Melissa Otto, head of research at S&P Global Visible Alpha.  Before the ​Iran war broke out, tech giants Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet and Meta planned to spend about $635 billion on data centres, chips, and other AI infrastructure in 2026, S&P Global has said.  Although tech companies have yet to signal cutbacks in those capital investments, persistently high oil prices could ⁠force spending revisions in the first and second quarters, bringing a "really meaningful correction in all equity markets," Otto said.

content.title: Big Tech's $635 billion AI spending faces energy shock test, S&P Global says
content.summary: Massive investments in artificial intelligence that underpinned record runs in equities face a major ‌hurdle as the Middle East crisis clouds prospects for growth ‌and energy costs, said Melissa Otto, head of research at S&P Global Visible Alpha.  Before the ​Iran war broke out, tech giants Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet and Meta planned to spend about $635 billion on data centres, chips, and other AI infrastructure in 2026, S&P Global has said.  Although tech companies have yet to signal cutbacks in those capital investments, persistently high oil prices could ⁠force spending revisions in the first and second quarters, bringing a "really meaningful correction in all equity markets," Otto said.

headline: Senior Researchers From NVIDIA, Samsung, NASA, University of Toronto Join AXL to Bring Global AI Expertise to Canadian Venture Creation
summary: TORONTO, March 31, 2026--AXL, a Canadian venture studio transforming applied AI research into high-growth companies, has announced its inaugural cohort of Faculty Fellows, a group of nine distinguished researchers, all professors at the University of Toronto (U of T), whose work spans computer vision, machine learning, and beyond at some of the world’s leading organizations.

headline: Microsoft’s New AI Agents Tie Copilot And Azure Deeper Into Workflows
summary: Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT) has introduced Critique and Council for Copilot, bringing a multi model and cross model comparison approach to its enterprise AI assistant. New autonomous business execution platforms, such as Gieni ABX, are being built entirely on Microsoft Azure to run end to end business workflows. These launches and ecosystem deployments highlight a shift toward AI agents that handle full operational tasks rather than serving only as productivity helpers. For investors watching...

headline: AVUS Outpaced VTI by 16 Points Over 5 Years. Is It the Better Core Holding?
summary: Most broad U.S. equity ETFs just buy the market. Avantis U.S. Equity ETF (NYSEARCA:AVUS) does something subtler: it buys most of the market, then tilts the portfolio toward stocks that are cheaper relative to their fundamentals and more profitable than their peers. The result resembles an index fund but behaves differently in practice. What AVUS ... AVUS Outpaced VTI by 16 Points Over 5 Years. Is It the Better Core Holding?
